segments.text: I loathe my very life; therefore I will give free rein to my complaint and speak out in the bitterness of my soul.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do not declare me guilty, but tell me what charges you have against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Does it please you to oppress me, to spurn the work of your hands, while you smile on the plans of the wicked?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do you have eyes of flesh? Do you see as a mortal sees?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Are your days like those of a mortal or your years like those of a strong man,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: that you must search out my faults and probe after my sin—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: though you know that I am not guilty and that no one can rescue me from your hand?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Your hands shaped me and made me. Will you now turn and destroy me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Remember that you molded me like clay. Will you now turn me to dust again?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Did you not pour me out like milk and curdle me like cheese,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: clothe me with skin and flesh and knit me together with bones and sinews?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You gave me life and showed me kindness, and in your providence watched over my spirit.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But this is what you concealed in your heart, and I know that this was in your mind:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I sinned, you would be watching me and would not let my offense go unpunished.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I am guilty—woe to me! Even if I am innocent, I cannot lift my head, for I am full of shame and drowned in my affliction.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I hold my head high, you stalk me like a lion and again display your awesome power against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You bring new witnesses against me and increase your anger toward me; your forces come against me wave upon wave.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why then did you bring me out of the womb? I wish I had died before any eye saw me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If only I had never come into being, or had been carried straight from the womb to the grave!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Are not my few days almost over? Turn away from me so I can have a moment's joy
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: before I go to the place of no return, to the land of gloom and utter darkness,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: to the land of deepest night, of utter darkness and disorder, where even the light is like darkness.
segments.speaker: Job
